extra.reasoning:
The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds on every reachable execution.

Analysis of how each variable changes:
- sent becomes TRUE only via [t_send_allowed] on send_attempt, whose guard is `not opted_out`. There is no other transition anywhere that sets sent := true.
- opted_out becomes TRUE only via [t_optout] on optout, and that same atomic update also sets sent := false. No transition ever sets opted_out back to false, so opted_out is monotonic (once true, stays true).
- Every other transition — all stage adv_*/rst_* transitions and [t_send_blocked] — sets sent := false (or leaves it false), never true.

To violate the property we would need a reachable state with opted_out = true AND sent = true. Two cases:
1. opted_out becomes true while sent is true: impossible, because the only transition setting opted_out := true (t_optout) simultaneously sets sent := false. So right after any optout, sent is false.
2. sent becomes true after opted_out is already true: impossible, because the only transition that sets sent := true is t_send_allowed, guarded by `not opted_out`. When opted_out is true the only enabled send transition is t_send_blocked, which sets sent := false. The stage/combination-lock transitions never set sent := true and never gate or unblock the send.

The lock stages (key_2 advances stage 0->1->2, key_1 advances 2->3 via adv_2 wait—actually adv_2 is key_1 at stage2->3, adv_3 key_0 3->4, adv_4 key_1 4->5, adv_5 key_1 5->6; resets on the off-keys) are irrelevant to the property: stage never appears in the guards of t_send_allowed, t_send_blocked, or t_optout. The lock only ever forces sent := false.

Therefore the unsafe combination (opted_out=true, sent=true) is unreachable on any execution length. No counterexample exists within 9 events (or any length). The property holds.
